title: qwirq.yaml
description: QWIRQ project manifest: how a project presents and runs on QWIRQ. The build pipeline (P0-c) parses this into a registry AppManifest + runner entries; VS Code validates against it (DX-5). See DEV-EXPERIENCE-PLAN.md sections 3 and 6.

id.description: Stable app id within the instance (kebab-case). Becomes AppManifest.id / instance_apps.app_id.

icon.description: Icon key the shell maps to an icon component.

nav.description: Where the app mounts in the shell nav. Omit to use defaults.

hidden.description: Registered + mountable but not shown in the sidebar (AppManifest.nav=false).

requires.description: PDP gate (AppManifest.requires); omitted = always visible. Evaluated server-side per user.

provides.description: The capabilities this project provides (at least one). A project can provide several.

app.description: A UI surface mounted in the shell. React, built against the instance-pinned React (Module Federation remote).

entry.description: Path to the app entry module, e.g. src/app/index.tsx.

functions.description: Server functions: backend logic run in the in-instance runner, served at POST /api/apps/{id}/fn/{name} (contract v1; see the 'Server functions' article in the knowledge base).

name.description: Stable function identifier within the app; part of the invocation URL.

entry.description: Path to the handler module (default-exports async (ctx, input) => json).

http.description: Reserved: REST-style alias, e.g. 'POST /assign'. Accepted now; alias routing is a fast-follow. The canonical /fn/{name} route always works.

tool.description: Declare this function as a Mabel tool (F-010). Calls arrive as actor 'mabel' on behalf of an authenticated user; authorization evaluates as that user. Declaration is v1-stable; Mabel execution is a fast-follow.

description.description: What the tool does, written for the assistant.

parameters.description: JSON Schema for the tool's input (also documents the function's input shape).

secrets.description: Vault secret NAMES this function may read at runtime via ctx.secrets.get(name). Declared-only: the function reads exactly these, resolved AS THE INVOKING USER (only the user's own grants apply — never blanket vault access). An undeclared name throws before any DB call. Omit = the function reads no secrets.

jobs.description: Scheduled or triggered runs (the runner model).

schedule.description: Cron expression for scheduled runs, e.g. '0 2 * * *'.

migrations.description: Path to the migrations directory (fed to `qwirq data migrate`).

runtime.description: Backend runtime host. P0 supports node20; python and rust are a fast-follow (DX-8) and will extend this enum. UI is always React/TS regardless.

platform.description: The instance platform release this builds against (pins React + the SDK), e.g. '2026.06'.
